narration: The second you drove your new car off the dealership lot, it lost about $9,600 in value. Not in a year — instantly.
visual: Car SVG rolling off dealership lot, dollar bills flying off the vehicle. '-$9,600' counter drops in red. Dealership building in background.

narration: New cars depreciate roughly 20% in their very first year. On the average $48,000 vehicle, that's nearly ten grand — vanished before your first oil change.
visual: Car with '$48,000' price tag, then a large 20% orange slice cuts out of the car's value. '$9,600 GONE' flashes below on light background.

narration: By year three it gets worse. Half the car's original value has evaporated — over $24,000 handed to time and the dealership, not you.
visual: Timeline bar from Year 0 to Year 3, car icon above, value bar shrinking from $48K to $24K. Red shading fills the lost portion.

narration: Here's the flip side — a two-year-old certified pre-owned car has already absorbed that depreciation hit. Someone else took the loss. Not you.
visual: Two cars side by side: 'NEW $48K' left, 'CPO 2YR $37K' right with green checkmark. Arrow points to the right car. Savings badge: 'SAVE $11K'.

narration: Buy new five times across your lifetime, and you've quietly surrendered over eighty thousand dollars in pure depreciation. That's a retirement account.
visual: Five car icons in a row on dark background, each shedding a red money stack. Counter builds to '$80,000+'. Retirement piggy bank comparison.

narration: Always buy two to three years used. Let the first owner pay the new-car tax. That one rule saves six figures over your lifetime.
visual: Two path arrows: left 'BUY NEW' leading to shrinking wallet, right 'BUY 2-YR USED' leading to growing piggy bank. 'SAVE $80K+' CTA badge springs in.
